your_book:
July 1 opened with real energy in the growth names. ServiceNow surged +6.57% today — the single biggest daily mover in the book — adding meaningful mark-to-market to a position already up +11.0% from cost; at 14.7% of the portfolio it is the largest weight and it carried its share. Adobe followed with +2.91%, a welcome reprieve for a name that is still -14.0% from Jaleel's cost basis despite consistently strong fundamentals — the narrative headwind (AI-disruption fear, CFO exit) remains the real drag, not the numbers. Samsara (IOT) jumped +5.4% as well, though it sits on an AVOID verdict with a QARP of 50.1, so that bounce doesn't change the thesis problem. The defensive core — Abbott, Zoetis, Danaher — drifted quietly higher between +0.82% and +1.70%, doing exactly what capital-preservation anchors should. On the downside, Rocket Lab slipped -1.55% and Novartis -1.65%; both are in the red from cost (-5.1% and -5.3% respectively), and neither has a compelling verdict (AVOID and HOLD-QUAL) to justify patience.

The clearest thesis drift remains in Jaleel's two elevated-risk names. Boston Scientific is -10.1% from cost while carrying a STRONG BUY / QARP 83.3 — the quality score says hold, but the slashed guidance (FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5%, management guiding flat through Q3) means price and thesis are moving in opposite directions; Q2 earnings around July 23 are the forcing function. Lululemon sits -9.0% from cost on a BUY verdict, yet the underlying data — five straight quarters of negative Americas comps, tariff margin pressure, stalled China — reads more like a structural downgrade in progress than a temporary rough patch. The verdict hasn't caught up to the flag.

Zoetis deserves a quiet watch too: -8.0% from cost, STRONG BUY, QARP 84.6 — the quality case is intact but the gap between the score and the price action is widening and has not been explained by any flag in today's data. On KO, unusual options flow (Jul 17 $83 calls, 2,061 contracts near the ask vs. 362 OI) is worth noting ahead of July 28 earnings — small position, but it's a data point. Portfolio is sitting on a -$360 / -1.75% unrealized loss overall with $2.49 in cash, leaving zero dry powder to act on anything.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, -10.1% from cost, earnings ~Jul 23 — thesis or exit decision due
attention.note: FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3; with Q2 results ~3 weeks away, Jaleel needs to decide whether the STRONG BUY verdict still holds or whether the position should be trimmed before the next potential disappointment.

attention.headline: Five straight negative comps, tariff margin hit — BUY verdict lags deteriorating fundamentals
attention.note: The risk flag cites Americas comps at -5% for a fifth consecutive quarter, gutted margins from tariffs, and a stalled China business; the BUY verdict and the on-the-ground data are increasingly hard to reconcile and next earnings are ~Sep 4.

attention.headline: AVOID verdict, -5.1% from cost, no thesis support — why is this still held?
attention.note: Rocket Lab carries the book's only AVOID verdict alongside a QARP of 37.0 and is down on the day and from cost; absent a documented reason for holding through the verdict, this position is a drag with no visible path to resolution.

attention.headline: Unusual call sweep ahead of Jul 28 earnings — small position but elevated options activity
attention.note: A sweep of 2,061 Jul 17 $83 calls near the ask vs. only 362 open interest flags unusual directional positioning roughly four weeks before KO reports; the position is only 0.8% of the book but the signal is worth tracking.
